broker_fee_detail: NO BROKER FEE stated; listed by Voro Purple LLC (out-of-state principal office)
broker_message: Hi Edwin — I'm interested in 315 61st St #D9 for the 8/1 move-in. Since it's rent-stabilized, could you share the DHCR rent history confirming the $1,974.71 legal rent, confirm there's no broker fee, and total move-in costs? Also, who handles repairs locally, and can you confirm the photos are the actual D9 unit? Thanks!
broker_questions: Can I see the DHCR rent history confirming the $1,974.71 legal rent and no overcharge?
Who manages/repairs locally (brokerage is out of state)?
Why has this unit relisted several times since April 2026?
How reliable is winter heat/hot water?
What floor is D9 exactly, full 4-flight walk-up?
Do current photos reflect the actual D9 unit?
building_risk_rationale: Total HPD (~12) well below the 0.81/unit benchmark, spotless 5-yr bedbug/rodent record, zero evictions, most violations resolved. But modestly above Sunset Park avgs on 311 (15), DOB (4), ECB (3 x $2,500 for unpermitted retaining-wall work) and a recurring heat/hot-water pattern (Jan-Feb 2026, Dec 2024, Nov 2019) plus a historical Class C 'no heat.' Exact open count inconsistent, unverified. Retaining-wall permit signed off.
commute_notes: Best transit of the strong picks: N/R/W hub at 59th St under 500 ft (~3 min). N express to Midtown ~35-40 min. R local also available.
concessions_to_ask: confirm DHCR legal rent in writing and no overcharge
confirm no broker fee and move-in cost
cons: Recurring winter heat/hot-water incl. a historical Class C 'no heat'
Out-of-state brokerage and repeated relists; no room to negotiate rent (at legal cap)
leverage_notes: Priced fairly vs Sunset Park 1BR comps and offered right at the legal regulated rent, so almost no room to negotiate down. Only real ask is a minor concession; the bigger win is confirming stabilization and no overcharge.
noise_risks: near 4th Ave / Gowanus Expwy — some traffic noise possible
owner_entity: Registered contact: Demetrius Partridge (Astoria); owner shown inconsistently
plain_english: Strongest all-around pick: a no-fee, rent-stabilized 1BR at $1,975 (right at its legal rent, below the Sunset Park median) with the best commute of the group — N express and R under 500 ft, ~35-40 min to Midtown. Low violation load, no bedbugs/evictions. Catches: 4th-floor walk-up, recurring winter heat, out-of-state brokerage that has relisted a few times. Little rent to negotiate (at legal cap). Worth touring; confirm DHCR legal rent, winter heat, and local repairs, then likely apply.
pros: Rent-stabilized 1BR at $1,975 — at legal rent and below the Sunset Park median, no fee
Excellent transit: N express + R at 59th St under 500 ft, ~35-40 min to Midtown
Low absolute violation count, no bedbugs/evictions; laundry + live-in super

red_flags.how_to_verify: Request DHCR rent history to confirm the legal rent and no overcharge.
red_flags.issue: Advertised rent ($1,975) is at/just above the stated legal rent ($1,974.71)
red_flags.why: Offered right at the legal cap — no preferential cushion, little room to be found illegally high.

red_flags.how_to_verify: Climb it on tour; confirm floor.
Confirm who manages/repairs locally; ask why it relisted.
red_flags.issue: Out-of-state brokerage; unit relisted repeatedly since April
red_flags.why: Remote brokerage can mean slower local response.

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask super; check HPD Online; talk to tenants.
red_flags.issue: Recurring heat/hot-water complaints + historical Class C 'no heat'
red_flags.why: 1924 walk-up with repeated winter heat emergencies, esp. top floor.

unverified: DHCR legal rent $1,974.71 and no overcharge
owner/management identity and local repair contact
unit sqft/floor plan and whether photos are the actual D9
